findings.title: streamFromAiSdk: 94 lines of new streaming logic with zero tests
findings.description: The new streamFromAiSdk function is the entire point of this feature (AI SDK migration) and contains complex logic: chunk type dispatching (text-delta, tool-call, tool-result), promise settlement with resolveOnce/rejectOnce, finish reason mapping (note: AI SDK uses 'tool-calls' with a hyphen vs xsAI's 'tool_calls' with underscore), error propagation via onError callback, and a fallback resolution via result.text promise. None of this is tested. This is the critical path for the feature -- if it doesn't work, the entire AI SDK migration is broken. The chunk.type mapping and finishReason string difference ('tool-calls' vs 'tool_calls') are especially bug-prone without tests.
findings.suggestion: Add integration tests for streamFromAiSdk that verify: (1) text-delta chunks are forwarded correctly via onStreamEvent, (2) tool-call chunks are converted with JSON.stringify(chunk.input) for args, (3) tool-result chunks forward toolCallId and output, (4) finishReason 'tool-calls' with waitForTools=true does NOT resolve the promise, (5) error propagation via onError rejects the promise, (6) the result.text fallback resolution works when onFinish does not fire. Use a real AI SDK endpoint via environment-gated test (similar to existing llm.test.ts pattern) or a Test Double with documented rationale.

findings.title: onError handler missing try/catch around onStreamEvent callback
findings.description: The onError handler in streamFromAiSdk calls `options?.onStreamEvent?.(...)` without a try/catch, unlike the onChunk (line 146) and onFinish (line 171) handlers which both wrap their onStreamEvent calls in try/catch. If the onStreamEvent callback throws, the error propagates as an unhandled rejection from the AI SDK callback context, and `rejectOnce(error)` is never reached -- meaning the wrapping Promise never settles (hangs forever).

findings.title: useAiSdk flag stored but never passed to llmStore.stream()
findings.code_snippet: const useAiSdk = useLocalStorageManualReset<boolean>('settings/consciousness/use-ai-sdk', false)
findings.description: The consciousness store exposes a `useAiSdk` boolean setting (line 15, exported line 85), and the LLM store accepts `useAiSdk?: boolean` in StreamOptions (llm.ts line 28) to branch between xsAI and AI SDK streaming. However, the primary caller in chat.ts (line 296) does NOT pass `useAiSdk` in the options object. Similarly, the event-handler-spark-notify caller omits it. This means the user can toggle the setting in the UI, but it has zero effect -- the AI SDK path is unreachable. This is a classic orphan-wiring issue: the setting exists, the consumer exists, but nothing connects them.
findings.suggestion: In packages/stage-ui/src/stores/chat.ts where llmStore.stream() is called, read the consciousness store's useAiSdk value and pass it as `useAiSdk: consciousnessStore.useAiSdk` in the StreamOptions. Do the same for any other callers (event-handler-spark-notify). This must be wired before this feature is considered functional.

findings.title: StreamEvent 'finish' variant uses `& any`, destroying type safety
findings.code_snippet: | ({ type: 'finish' } & any)
findings.description: Encapsulation: N/A, Expression: 3/10, Usefulness: 5/10, Enforcement: 2/10 (Aggregate: 3.3). The StreamEvent discriminated union has `{ type: 'finish' } & any` for its finish variant. Intersecting with `any` erases all type information -- the compiler will accept any property access on finish events without error. This defeats the purpose of a discriminated union, which is to make illegal states unrepresentable. The finish event should have explicit fields (e.g., `finishReason: string`) rather than falling back to `any`. This is pre-existing code, but the new `streamFromAiSdk` function relies on this type for its `onFinish` callback, so the unsafety propagates into the new code.
findings.suggestion: Replace `({ type: 'finish' } & any)` with a properly typed variant: `{ type: 'finish', finishReason: string }` (or whatever fields the finish event actually carries). This will restore compile-time checking for finish event consumers in both the xsAI and AI SDK streaming paths.

findings.title: stream() useAiSdk branch routing is untested
findings.description: The stream() method now branches between streamFrom and streamFromAiSdk based on the useAiSdk flag. This routing logic determines which SDK handles all LLM communication. The existing llm.test.ts only tests attemptForToolsCompatibilityDiscovery (which always uses the xsAI path). No test verifies that useAiSdk:true routes to the AI SDK path or that useAiSdk:false/undefined continues using xsAI.
findings.suggestion: Add tests for the stream() function that verify: (1) with useAiSdk: false, the xsAI streamText path is used, (2) with useAiSdk: true, the AI SDK streamText path is used. These should be integration tests with a real or environment-gated endpoint, not mocked routing.

findings.title: models() silently converts URL error to empty array
findings.description: When listModels fails due to an invalid URL, the error is caught and an empty array is returned. This makes it impossible for callers to distinguish between 'no models available' (legitimate empty response) and 'configuration error' (invalid API URL). No warning is logged. This is pre-existing code but is part of the diff scope and follows the 'error-as-valid-state' anti-pattern seen elsewhere in this codebase.

findings.title: resolveXsaiTools extracted function lacks dedicated tests
findings.description: resolveXsaiTools was extracted from the inline streamFrom logic and is now shared by both streamFrom and streamFromAiSdk. It has branching logic: tools can be a function or array, compatibility check gates whether tools are returned at all, and it merges mcp() + debug() + user tools. As shared infrastructure for both streaming paths, bugs here affect both old and new code. No test exercises this function directly or indirectly (the existing llm.test.ts tests attemptForToolsCompatibilityDiscovery which calls streamFrom which calls resolveXsaiTools, but only in the env-gated path that requires an API key).
findings.suggestion: Test resolveXsaiTools behavior: (1) returns undefined when supportsTools is false, (2) returns merged tools array when supportsTools is true, (3) handles tools as async function, (4) handles tools as direct array, (5) handles undefined tools option. Since this is a pure-ish function (depends on mcp()/debug() side effects), consider extracting the tool merging logic into a testable pure function.

findings.title: consciousness store useAiSdk state has no test coverage
findings.code_snippet: const useAiSdk = useLocalStorageManualReset<boolean>('settings/consciousness/use-ai-sdk', false)
findings.description: A new useAiSdk boolean state was added to the consciousness store, exposed in the return object (line 85). This is the user-facing toggle that controls whether the entire LLM streaming path uses xsAI or AI SDK. No test file exists for the consciousness store at all (confirmed by glob search). The state's default value (false), its localStorage persistence key, and its exposure through the store API are all untested.
findings.suggestion: Create packages/stage-ui/src/stores/modules/consciousness.test.ts that tests: (1) useAiSdk defaults to false, (2) useAiSdk persists to localStorage under the correct key 'settings/consciousness/use-ai-sdk', (3) useAiSdk is included in the store's returned state. Since this is a Pinia store with localStorage, test with a real Pinia instance and mock localStorage (localStorage is a browser API, not a Repository/Service -- this is an acceptable test double).

findings.title: Message type cast between xsAI and AI SDK may lose/corrupt data
findings.code_snippet: messages: sanitized as unknown as import('ai').ModelMessage[],
findings.description: The xsAI Message type is cast to AI SDK's ModelMessage[] via `as unknown as` double-cast. xsAI messages use `role: 'user' | 'assistant' | 'system'` with `content: string | ContentPart[]`, while AI SDK ModelMessage has a different structure (e.g., `role` enum differences, content parts schema differences, tool result encoding). The sanitizeMessages function converts 'error' role to 'user', but does not handle the structural differences between the two SDK message formats. This can cause silent data corruption or runtime errors when multi-modal content parts, tool call results, or assistant messages with tool invocations are passed to the AI SDK path.
findings.suggestion: Create an explicit `convertXsaiMessagesToAiSdk(messages: Message[]): import('ai').ModelMessage[]` adapter function in use-ai-sdk.ts that maps each message role and content format correctly, rather than using an unsafe double cast. Include unit tests for edge cases (tool call messages, multi-modal content, system messages).

findings.title: resolveXsaiTools I/O calls (mcp/debug) lack error wrapping
findings.code_snippet:
return [
  ...await mcp(),
  ...await debug(),
  ...await resolveTools(),
]
findings.description: The resolveXsaiTools function calls three async I/O operations (mcp(), debug(), resolveTools()) without any try/catch or error wrapping. If mcp() or debug() fails (e.g., MCP server unreachable), the raw error propagates without context about which tool source failed or what model/provider was being configured. Both streamFrom (line 51) and streamFromAiSdk (line 131) call this function before their promise constructors, so the error surfaces as a rejection but with no operational context (model name, provider, which tool source failed).

findings.title: waitForTools comment only describes xsAI finish reason string
findings.code_snippet: waitForTools?: boolean // when true, won't resolve on finishReason=='tool_calls'
findings.description: The inline comment states the finish reason string is 'tool_calls' (underscore), which matches the xsAI streaming path (line 73). However, the new AI SDK streaming path (line 174) uses 'tool-calls' (hyphen) as the finish reason. Since both code paths read this same waitForTools option, the comment is incomplete and will mislead someone debugging the AI SDK path into expecting the wrong finish reason string.
findings.suggestion: Update comment to reflect both variants: `// when true, won't resolve on tool-related finishReason ('tool_calls' for xsAI, 'tool-calls' for AI SDK)`

findings.title: Duplicate settled/resolveOnce/rejectOnce pattern across stream functions
findings.description: The settled/resolveOnce/rejectOnce pattern appears identically in both streamFrom (lines 54-66) and streamFromAiSdk (lines 135-137). This is ~10 lines of identical promise-settling logic duplicated across two functions.
findings.suggestion: Extract a createSettledPromise() utility function that returns { promise, resolveOnce, rejectOnce }. Both stream functions use this helper, eliminating duplication and making the settle-once guarantee centrally testable.

findings.title: streamFromAiSdk exceeds 50-line threshold at 73 lines
findings.description: streamFromAiSdk is 73 lines long with a maximum nesting depth of 4 levels. The function handles three concerns: (1) model/tool setup, (2) promise settling infrastructure, and (3) the streaming call with event mapping. Separating the event-mapping callbacks would reduce both length and nesting.
findings.suggestion: Extract a createAiSdkChunkHandler function for the onChunk callback. This reduces streamFromAiSdk to ~40 lines and makes the chunk-type mapping logic independently testable.

findings.title: API key and baseURL fall back to empty string silently
findings.code_snippet:
baseURL: String(chatConfig.baseURL ?? ''),
apiKey: String(chatConfig.apiKey ?? ''),
findings.description: In streamFromAiSdk, if chatConfig.baseURL or chatConfig.apiKey are undefined/null, they silently default to empty strings. An empty baseURL will cause a malformed URL. An empty apiKey will cause a 401 that manifests as a generic stream error. The empty-string fallback makes debugging harder.
findings.suggestion: Add fail-fast validation before creating the model: `if (!chatConfig.baseURL) throw new Error('AI SDK stream requires a baseURL in the chat provider config'); if (!chatConfig.apiKey) throw new Error('AI SDK stream requires an apiKey in the chat provider config');`

findings.title: AiSdkModelConfig.provider is bare `string` instead of literal union
findings.code_snippet: provider?: string
findings.description: The `provider` field in AiSdkModelConfig is typed as `string | undefined`, but the implementation only branches on `'anthropic'` vs everything-else. Passing `provider: 'gooogle'` (typo) compiles silently and falls through to the OpenAI path, which would produce confusing runtime errors.
findings.suggestion: Type the field as a literal union: `provider?: 'anthropic' | 'openai'`. This makes the valid values self-documenting and gives compile-time feedback on typos.

findings.title: onChunk callback has 4-level nesting with if/else if chain
findings.description: The onChunk handler at nesting level 4 uses a chain of if/else if to dispatch on chunk.type. Each branch is structurally identical. Using guard-clause style (if + return) instead of else-if would flatten the logic.
findings.suggestion: Replace else-if chain with guard clauses using early returns: `if (chunk.type === 'text-delta') return options?.onStreamEvent?.(...)`. This makes each branch independent and slightly reduces nesting.

findings.title: No integration test proving AI SDK stream path works end-to-end
findings.code_snippet:
describe('convertXsaiToolsToAiSdk', () => { ... })
describe('createAiSdkModel', () => { ... })
findings.description: The test file covers tool conversion and model creation (pure adapter functions), but there is no integration test proving that streamFromAiSdk actually works with a real or test-double AI SDK provider. The orchestration in streamFromAiSdk is where bugs will hide (message format mismatches, event mapping errors, promise settlement races).
findings.suggestion: Add at least one integration test for streamFromAiSdk using a Test Double AI SDK provider (with `// Test Double rationale: AI SDK provider is an external LLM API` comment). Verify that: (1) text-delta events are emitted, (2) tool-call events contain correct args, (3) the promise resolves on finish, (4) errors reject the promise.

findings.title: createAiSdkModel accepts empty baseURL/apiKey without validation
findings.description: When chatConfig.baseURL or chatConfig.apiKey is null/undefined, they are silently converted to empty strings. The createAiSdkModel function then creates an OpenAI/Anthropic client with empty credentials. This defers the failure to the network call where the error message will be confusing.

findings.title: Multiple `as unknown as` casts bypass type system at SDK boundary
findings.code_snippet:
const chatConfig = chatProvider.chat(model) as unknown as Record<string, unknown>
...
messages: sanitized as unknown as import('ai').ModelMessage[]
findings.description: The streamFromAiSdk function contains three unsafe double-casts (`as unknown as X`) to bridge between xsAI and AI SDK types. Double-casting through `unknown` silences all compiler warnings. If either SDK changes its types, these casts will silently produce runtime errors.
findings.suggestion: Create typed adapter functions: `extractChatConfig(provider: ...)` and `convertMessages(messages: Message[]): ModelMessage[]` that do explicit field mapping with Valibot validation rather than casting.

findings.title: streamFromAiSdk fallback resolution lacks error context
findings.code_snippet: Promise.resolve(result.text).then(() => resolveOnce()).catch(rejectOnce)
findings.description: The fallback resolution path catches errors from `result.text` and passes them directly to `rejectOnce` without any wrapping or context. If the AI SDK's text promise rejects, the caller receives a raw, potentially opaque error with no indication that it came from the stream text resolution fallback path.
findings.suggestion:
Wrap the error with context:

Promise.resolve(result.text)
  .then(() => resolveOnce())
  .catch(err => rejectOnce(new Error('AI SDK stream text resolution failed', { cause: err })))

findings.title: use-ai-sdk.test.ts missing error/edge case tests for createAiSdkModel
findings.description: createAiSdkModel tests cover happy paths well but miss boundary conditions: empty string baseURL, empty string apiKey, unknown provider string, and trailing slash normalization edge cases.
findings.suggestion: Add edge case tests: (1) baseURL with trailing slash is not double-slashed, (2) empty baseURL behavior, (3) empty apiKey behavior, (4) unknown provider string defaults to OpenAI path. These are pure function tests requiring no mocks.

findings.title: JSDoc references 'AI SDK 6' version that will rot
findings.description: Both JSDoc comments reference 'AI SDK 6' by major version number. When the SDK updates, these comments will become stale and misleading.
findings.suggestion: Replace 'AI SDK 6' with 'AI SDK' (unversioned). The package.json dependency already captures the actual version.

findings.title: Exported interface AiSdkModelConfig lacks JSDoc
findings.code_snippet:
export interface AiSdkModelConfig {
  baseURL: string
  apiKey: string
  modelId: string
  headers?: Record<string, string>
  provider?: string
}
findings.description: The exported AiSdkModelConfig interface has no JSDoc comment. The 'provider' field especially deserves documentation since only 'anthropic' is a recognized value.
findings.suggestion: Add JSDoc describing the config shape. Document 'provider' to clarify that only 'anthropic' triggers the Anthropic SDK path and all other values use the OpenAI-compatible path.

findings.title: streamFrom marginally exceeds 50-line threshold at 52 lines
findings.code_snippet: async function streamFrom(model: string, chatProvider: ChatProvider, messages: Message[], options?: StreamOptions) { ... }
findings.description: streamFrom is 52 lines, just over the 50-line guideline. Would be resolved automatically by extracting the settled-promise utility from finding merged-012.
findings.suggestion: Extract the settled promise pattern as described in finding merged-012, which would reduce this function to approximately 40 lines.

findings.title: StreamOptions.useAiSdk boolean flag controls code path dispatch
findings.code_snippet: useAiSdk?: boolean
findings.description: Adding `useAiSdk?: boolean` to StreamOptions creates a boolean flag that dispatches between two entirely different streaming implementations. For a feature-flag-style migration this is pragmatically reasonable but is a mild type design smell.
findings.suggestion: Consider creating separate entry points: `streamViaXsai()` and `streamViaAiSdk()` instead of routing via boolean flag. Alternatively, document the flag's intended removal timeline.

findings.title: Provider detection in createAiSdkModel uses only string match
findings.description: createAiSdkModel uses a simple string equality check for provider detection, defaulting to OpenAI-compatible. Adequate for two-provider setup but will need extension.
findings.suggestion: Consider adding a provider registry pattern when a third provider is needed, and validate that unknown provider values produce a clear error rather than silently using the OpenAI fallback.

positive_observations: Clean adapter pattern: convertXsaiToolsToAiSdk is a pure function with no side effects, well-documented, and thoroughly unit tested with 6 test cases covering edge cases.
Feature flag approach: useAiSdk is gated behind a localStorage setting defaulting to false, making the migration opt-in and safe to ship incrementally.
Consistent promise settlement pattern: Both streamFrom and streamFromAiSdk use the settled/resolveOnce/rejectOnce guard pattern to prevent double resolution.
Good refactoring: resolveXsaiTools was extracted as a shared helper, eliminating duplication between the two streaming paths.
Catalog-based dependency management: AI SDK packages added via pnpm catalog with pinned versions ensures consistency across the monorepo.
Three TODO/FIXME comments were removed and replaced with proper descriptive comments (sanitizeMessages, tool incompatibility errors, tools parameter).
The defensive comment on line 187 explains a non-obvious fallback mechanism -- a good 'why' comment that will help future maintainers.
use-ai-sdk.test.ts uses direct instantiation with real xsaiTool() objects -- no mocks, no fakes, proper Functional Core testing.
No mock violations detected in the test file (no jest.fn, vi.fn, vi.mock, InMemory*, Mock*, Fake* patterns).
Test file uses real xsAI tool() builder with zod schemas, proving the actual conversion pipeline works end-to-end.
Guard clause pattern used effectively in createAiSdkModel (early return for anthropic provider).
consciousness.ts change is minimal and clean -- a single reactive ref addition with no complexity increase.
The sanitizeMessages function is short, pure, and well-commented.
AiSdkModelConfig interface cleanly separates the configuration concerns from the model creation logic.
The settled flag pattern (let settled = false) is a well-implemented guard against race conditions in promise resolution.
onChunk and onFinish handlers correctly wrap onStreamEvent callbacks in try/catch, forwarding errors to rejectOnce.
